user_prompt: does extreme size make a difference? Analyze and create a Word report

source_data_summary.ground_truth: Planted anomaly: South/Product_B March Revenue 7,531 (80% drop from Feb 37,655). Organic: South/Product_E Aug Revenue 20,000.

agent_plan: Setting up to analyze sales data, focusing on identifying anomalies and their impact. Will compute totals and averages with and without outliers.

steps.latency_observed: approx 25 seconds — silent failure on first load, ran diagnostics (cwd, glob, hello-world), explicit openpyxl import on third attempt
steps.output: 216 rows × 5 columns loaded on third attempt. Month ordering applied.

steps.latency_observed: approx 35 seconds — most complex computation, multiple sequential blocks
steps.output: Total revenue 8,315,392. Extreme anomaly: South/Product_B Mar Revenue 7,531 (group_z=-14.80, typical median 42,468). South/Product_E Aug Revenue 20,000 (group_z=-4.11). Three scenarios: As-Reported 8,315,392, Drop-Anomalies 8,287,861 (-0.33%), Impute-to-Median 8,365,455 (+0.60%). South Jul→Aug MoM flips: +0.09% as-reported vs -9.48% if dropped vs +7.33% if imputed.
key_facts_produced: anomaly_1: Mar | South | Product_B | Revenue 7,531 | group_z=-14.80 | typical_median=42,468
anomaly_2: Aug | South | Product_E | Revenue 20,000 | group_z=-4.11 | typical_median=35,126

steps.output: 3 sensitivity charts generated. All computed facts including 3-scenario tables and South MoM sensitivity passed into Word context.

tools_called: python-docx Document, add_heading, add_paragraph, add_table, add_picture
steps.output: 6-section Word document. Section directly answers user question: 'Do extreme values make a difference?' Three sensitivity tables embedded. Dual-line charts showing as-reported vs imputed.
key_facts_produced: anomaly_section_title: Do extreme values make a difference?

steps.output: Narrative directly answers question: 'Yes — not much on full-year totals, but a lot on regional/monthly narratives.' Names both anomalies with reported vs typical comparisons. South Jul→Aug MoM flip quantified.
key_facts_produced: exec_summary: Total revenue 8,315,392. Two South anomalies distort MoM story. South Jul→Aug MoM: +0.09% as-reported, -9.48% if treated as error, +7.33% if normalised.
anomaly_answer: Yes — not much on full-year totals, but a lot on regional/monthly narratives.
anomaly_1_narrative: South/Product_B Mar Revenue 7,531 vs typical 42,468.
anomaly_2_narrative: South/Product_E Aug Revenue 20,000 vs typical 35,126.
recommendation_1: Validate South records with source system.
recommendation_3: Use robust KPIs (median, trimmed mean).
recommendation_4: Operational follow-up for real events or ETL fix.

word_output_actual_text.executive_summary: Total revenue 8,315,392. Two South anomalies distort MoM story. South Jul→Aug MoM: +0.09% as-reported, -9.48% if treated as error, +7.33% if normalised.
word_output_actual_text.anomaly_section: Yes — not much on full-year totals, but a lot on regional/monthly narratives. South/Product_B Mar Revenue 7,531 vs typical 42,468. South/Product_E Aug Revenue 20,000 vs typical 35,126.
recommendations: Validate South records with source system.
Add automated anomaly monitoring at Region×Product level.
Use robust KPIs for leadership reporting.
Operational follow-up or ETL fix if data errors confirmed.
